title: Requests Response
description: JSON Schema representing the key attributes of a requests.Response object returned by the Python Requests library after an HTTP call.

status_code.description: HTTP status code of the response (e.g., 200, 404, 500).

ok.description: True if status_code is less than 400, False otherwise.

url.description: Final URL of the response after following any redirects.

encoding.description: Encoding to use when accessing response.text. Guessed from Content-Type header.

text.description: Response body decoded as a string using response.encoding.

content.description: Response body as raw bytes (base64-encoded for JSON representation).

json_body.description: Parsed JSON body, equivalent to calling response.json(). Only present when Content-Type is application/json.

elapsed.description: Time elapsed between sending the request and receiving the last byte of the response, in seconds.

history.description: List of Response objects representing any redirects followed.

items.description: A redirect response in the redirect chain.

cookies.description: CookieJar of cookies the server sent back.

reason.description: HTTP reason phrase for the status code (e.g., 'OK', 'Not Found').

request.description: The PreparedRequest object that was sent.
